FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Prediction markets hit record volumes on World Cup betting, while Macquarie pivots to Chinese AI chips as a key growth thesis.

bullets.text: Prediction market platforms Kalshi, Polymarket, and newcomer Rothera smashed trading records in June on World Cup volumes, with Rothera alone moving $2B.
Macquarie initiates coverage on five Chinese AI chip makers with buy ratings, positioning itself as the lead voice on sector consolidation and growth.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: No stories in this lane today.

PE & Deals.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Markets & Macro.lede: PE deal faces shareholder revolt, tech valuations crack, and labor market softens—signaling potential rate-cut window amid concentrated market gains.

bullets.text: KKR/Bridgepoint's £5.7bn DCC Energy bid hits headwinds: Ninety One, Aviva Investors, and Fidelity International opposing, raising deal execution risk.
June jobs miss (217k vs 272k revised) weakens labor market signal, potentially opening Fed rate-cut door and supporting bond valuations.
Tech rally masking structural shift: AI trades concentrating returns while market breadth narrows and bond yields reset lower—old-school valuation discipline likely to outperform.

Newsletters.lede: Advisors question index strategy effectiveness while psilocybin emerges as a potential mental health play with regulatory tailwinds.

bullets.text: Advisors ditching broad index funds due to performance drag from low-quality holdings diluting market leaders—potential shift toward active/concentrated strategies.
Psilocybin treatments gaining White House support for treatment-resistant depression, but clinical efficacy and FDA pathway remain murky—early-stage biotech opportunity with execution risk.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Mostly macro and tech today: reflections on American dominance, AI's productivity acceleration, and a construction/policy roundup.

bullets.text: AI tools are collapsing the cost of intellectual exploration—turning weeks of preliminary work into hours—which lowers the bar for which ideas actually get developed and could reshape R&D productivity.
Noah Smith argues American economic/political dominance over 250 years directly enabled broad human flourishing, positioning U.S. leadership as causal rather than coincidental to global prosperity.

Policy/infrastructure watch: uninsured households rising, AI chip export controls tightening, Japan experimenting with dual-frequency grids, and Meta doubling down on AI compute—mix of risk and capex signals.

all.lede: PE deal friction in Europe, tech valuation reset underway, and softer labor data fueling bond optimism amid market breadth concerns.

bullets.text: KKR/Bridgepoint's £5.7bn DCC Energy bid facing shareholder revolt from Ninety One, Aviva, Fidelity—deal momentum at risk.
Tech valuations deteriorating sharply; FT argues old-fashioned valuation discipline will outperform as bubble deflates.
June jobs data (217k payrolls, down from 272k) signals softer labor market and lower inflation—opening door for Fed rate cuts and bond rally.
Market breadth collapsing under surface gains: AI trades concentrating returns while bond yields reset lower, marking regime shift.
